expectations.text: Uses 'obsidian files' or 'obsidian search' with a path/folder filter to list notes in 1-Projects/website-redesign/
expectations.evidence: Uses 'obsidian files format=paths | grep "^1-Projects/website-redesign/"' to list and filter files in the target folder.

expectations.text: Uses 'obsidian property:set file="..." name="status" value="complete"' (or correct equivalent syntax)
expectations.evidence: Uses 'obsidian property:set file="$note" name="status" value="complete"' inside the loop — exact correct syntax.

expectations.text: Uses 'obsidian property:set' to set the completed date to 2026-04-24
expectations.evidence: Uses 'obsidian property:set file="$note" name="completed" value="2026-04-24"' inside the same loop.

expectations.text: Provides a loop pattern to apply the property updates to all files in the folder
expectations.evidence: Uses a 'while read note; do ... done' shell loop piped from the filtered file list to apply both property updates to every file.

expectations.text: CLI parameter style is param=value, not --param
expectations.evidence: All CLI arguments use param=value style throughout: format=paths, file="$note", name="status", value="complete", name="completed", value="2026-04-24".

claims: property:set creates the property if it doesn't exist and overwrites if it does
The completed value follows Obsidian's Date property format (YYYY-MM-DD)
Obsidian must be running for CLI commands to work

uncertainties: Uses grep to filter paths rather than a native folder filter argument — works but relies on shell tooling outside the obsidian CLI
workarounds: No native 'obsidian files path=...' folder scoping available, so grep is used as a post-filter

suggestions: Consider testing whether 'obsidian search' with a path filter is available as a more idiomatic alternative to piping through grep
The verification step (Step 3) is a nice addition and demonstrates good practices for bulk operations
eval_feedback.overall: Perfect score — all 5 assertions pass. The output is clear, well-structured, and uses correct param=value CLI syntax throughout. The loop pattern is idiomatic shell and correctly applies both property updates per file. The use of grep as a folder filter is a reasonable workaround for the lack of native folder scoping in the obsidian files command.
